slides.title: Media, Technology, and News
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 7 English, Unit 8: Media, Technology, and News. Welcome to Unit 8. This unit is called "Media, Technology, and News" because you will explore how information travels in the modern world and why it matters to think critically about everything you read, watch, and share. Every day, millions of messages flow through newspapers, television channels, websites, and social media platforms. Some of these messages are carefully researched and truthful. Others are misleading, exaggerated, or completely false. As a young person growing up in the digital age, one of the most important skills you can develop is the ability to tell the difference between reliable information and misinformation. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Explain what media literacy means and why it is important for young people today
Identify the key features of a reliable news source and an unreliable one
Read a text about media and answer comprehension and analysis questions in full sentences
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to explain what media literacy means and why it is important for young people today. you will also identify the key features of a reliable news source and an unreliable one. you will also read a text about media and answer comprehension and analysis questions in full sentences. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: express, claim, allege, correspond, explanation, influence. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: Media, Technology, and News: source text 1
Media, Technology, and News: source text 2
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Media, Technology, and News: source text 1 and Media, Technology, and News: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Present Perfect + Just
bullets: What it means and why it matters The present perfect tense is used to talk about actions or experiences that happened at an unspecified time in the past...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Present Perfect + Just. What it means and why it matters The present perfect tense is used to talk about actions or experiences that happened at an unspecified time in the past but are important now. When we add just, we emphasise that something happened very recently. This is especially useful in news reporting, where journalists often say things like "The government has just announced a new policy" to show that the news is fresh and current. Understanding this structure will help you share news and describe recent... Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Debate: Digital Media Ethics. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
